mechanism_narrative: A more negative 3-month change in FEDFUNDS lowers equity discount rates and raises the present value of expected cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when easing is not interpreted as panic.
failure_condition: Reject if lower 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent low signals deteriorating household income and rising recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below 1000 thousand units signals a housing-led contraction in durable demand, credit creation, and construction employment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1000 episodes do not have lower subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than other months, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint 3-month declines in RSAFS and INDPRO indicate that final demand and production are contracting together, forcing earnings revisions lower and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both post negative 3-month growth
failure_condition: Reject if the joint negative-growth trigger has no incremental predictive power beyond RSAFS or INDPRO alone, or if average triggered 20-day returns are nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum is high while FEDFUNDS is low, real policy rates are compressed and inflation pressure limits further easing, raising margin and valuation risk and producing negative 5-day returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4% while FEDFUNDS is below 3%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction coefficient is not negative out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month PAYEMS growth signals expanding wage income and resilient corporate revenues, supporting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher PAYEMS 3-month growth fails to rank future 60-day S&P 500 returns positively, with Rank IC below 0.03 in two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in INDPRO relative to its 6-month average signals weakening factory output and operating leverage, causing near-term earnings-risk repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside-crossing dates are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below nontriggered returns.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS is being cut sharply, policy easing is interpreted as confirmation of recessionary demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth falls below 1% and FEDFUNDS 6-month change is less than -100bp
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 deceleration and FEDFUNDS-cut trigger produces average 60-day S&P 500 returns at or above zero, or if either single proxy explains all predictive power.
